intro: Tapinanthus angiensis, also known as mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that grows on the branches of various host trees, obtaining its nutrients by connecting to the host plant's vascular tissues. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce stress, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
